  "decision": "fail",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Random cracking observed due to late sawcut joints",
    "Sawcut joints not executed within the required time window per ACI 302/308"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Evaluate extent of random cracking and map crack patterns",
    "Determine if cracks compromise structural integrity or durability",
    "Submit repair procedure for engineer approval (e.g., epoxy injection, routing and sealing)",
    "Review and revise jointing plan and timing for remaining placements"
  ],
  "rationale": "Sawcut joints must be made as soon as the concrete can support the sawing without raveling, typically within 4-12 hours after finishing. Late sawcutting allows shrinkage stresses to exceed the concrete's tensile strength, resulting in random cracking. This is a defect per ACI 302 and 308, compromising durability and potentially structural performance.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 302.1R-15 (Guide to Concrete Floor and Slab Construction)",
    "ACI 308R-16 (Guide to External Curing of Concrete)"
  ]
}
